summary: Read and analyzed <machine-local-path> (1).md in full, verified expected SHA-256, and produced a Korean analysis focused on Codex/Claude parity, direct MCP canaries, pre-response/direct OS access, task session lifecycle, finish_task grounding, and wrong-task finish prevention. No requested files were edited.
decisions: Treated the task as read-only analysis; no source or plan file edits were made.
Concluded the plan is directionally strong but should tighten current-tool naming, pre-response proof, finish_task schema enforcement, and wrong-task finish rejection semantics.
Observed current DinoBrain MCP tool exposure uses start_task rather than the document's os_begin_task naming, making an alias or migration contract important for canary parity.
next_steps: Clarify whether os_begin_task is the intended new canonical tool name or an alias over start_task, then update parity canaries accordingly.
Promote pre-response context delivery and fail-closed behavior to a first-class gate with trace evidence before first substantive agent work.
Extend finish_task/task-session schemas so used evidence, prompt-turn binding, grounding classification, and wrong-task finish attempts are enforced server-side, not only reported later.
Move P1-16 earlier in the implementation order if validators or other packages depend on grounded task lifecycle evidence.
